items.title: Loting CL: City en Real wéér tegenover elkaar, Slot naar Istanbul
items.summary: De loting voor de achtste finale van de Champions League heeft vrijdag een aantal heerlijke krakers opgeleverd. Zo staan Manchester City en Real Madrid opnieuw tegenover elkaar, spelen Chelsea en Paris Saint-Germain de reprise van de finale van het WK voor clubs en krijgt het Liverpool van Arne Slot opnieuw te maken met Galatasaray.

items.title: Rondje Europa: AZ zorgt eindelijk weer voor broodnodige Nederlandse coëfficiënten
items.summary: AZ is door naar de volgende ronde van de Conference League. Na de blamage van een week geleden werd donderdagavond wél afgerekend met FC Noah: 4-0. Clubwatcher Simon Zwartkruis en Geert-Jan Jakobs praten uitgebreid na met presentator Kalum van Oudheusden.

items.title: Madrid vs City again in UCL draw, Chelsea get PSG
items.summary: Real Madrid have been handed another mouthwatering clash with Manchester City in the round-of-16 draw for the Champions League, while holders Paris Saint-Germain face Chelsea.

items.title: De Lange in gesprek met Marseille over nieuw contract
items.summary: Jeffrey de Lange is met Olympique Marseille in gesprek over een nieuw contract. Zijn huidige verbintenis loopt nog door tot medio 2027 en zijn Franse werkgever wil graag met hem verder.

items.title: Manchester City face Real Madrid in Champions League last-16 draw: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend ⚽ Champions League draw from 11am (GMT) | Mail Barry Football Association news: Kevin Thelwell has been appointed the FA’s Elite Coach Developer, tasked with supporting the progression of elite homegrown coaches. The former Rangers and Everton sporting director’s strategic role at St George’s Park will span men’s and women’s football and he will report to Dan Ashworth, the chief football officer at the national football centre. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: aftellen naar loting Europa League en Conference League
items.summary: Vrijdagmiddag wordt er geloot voor de achtste finales in de Champions League, de Europa League en de Conference League. In dat laatste toernooi verdwijnt AZ als enige Nederlandse club in de koker. De Alkmaarders treffen AEK Athene of Sparta Praag in de achtste finale. Via dit liveblog blijf je de hele middag op de hoogte van al het lotinggeweld.

items.title: Nottingham Forest turn to former Spurs head of medicine after team’s injury struggles
items.summary: Geoff Scott appointed in medical department overhaul He had 20 years at Spurs but clashed with Postecoglou Nottingham Forest has appointed a new director of performance, Tottenham’s former head of medicine and sports science Geoff Scott, amid concerns that injury problems have exacerbated their struggle to avoid relegation. Scott spent 20 years at Tottenham until leaving two years ago after a clash with the then manager, Ange Postecoglou, who was in charge of Forest for eight matches this season. Continue reading...

items.title: Klopp's job at risk? 'Nonsense,' says Red Bull
items.summary: Red Bull backed former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp to remain the drinks giant's head of global soccer and dismissed a media report that his job could be under threat after barely a year.

items.title: Liverpool back in profit, but wages highest in PL
items.summary: Liverpool returned to profit after their Premier League-winning season with a pre-tax surplus of £15.2million ($20.5) but had the highest wage bill.

items.title: Gordon told: Get Arsenal link 'out of your brain'
items.summary: Newcastle boss Eddie Howe has urged striker Anthony Gordon not to be distracted by speculation linking him with a summer move to Arsenal.

items.title: Neymar shushes critics after first goals in return
items.summary: Neymar broke his 2026 goal drought in spectacular fashion Thursday, leading Santos to a 2-1 victory over Vasco da Gama in the fourth round of the Brasileirão.

items.title: Waarom NAC wegloopt met de routine van aanvalsbrein Ayew (36)
items.summary: Zonder te scoren, maakt André Ayew (36) indruk op zijn collega's bij NAC Breda. De Ghanese recordinternational kwam deze winter verrassend naar het Rat Verlegh Stadion. Daar geldt hij als grote leermeester voor de andere spitsen.

items.title: Gordon needs to stay 'fully focused' after Arsenal talk - Howe
items.summary: Head coach Eddie Howe has urged Anthony Gordon to stay "fully focused" after the Newcastle United forward was linked with a move to Arsenal.

items.title: CL knockout draw LIVE! Real vs Man City, PSG vs Chelsea & Newcastle vs Barca
Liverpool’s title win last season fuelled by Premier League’s highest wage bill
items.summary: Wage bill increased by £42m to £428m, accounts show Revenue hit record £703m but profit was modest £8m Liverpool had the highest wage bill in the Premier League when winning their 20th league title last season, the club’s latest set of accounts have revealed. Liverpool’s wage bill increased by £42m to £428m in the year ending 31 May 2025, when a Premier League title triumph in Arne Slot’s debut season as head coach and a return to the Champions League increased revenue to a record £703m. The club’s wages-to-revenue ratio stood at a healthy 61%. It was the biggest wage bill in the division, ahead of Manchester City on £408m. Continue reading...

items.title: So much for three-year plan - what is the secret to manager longevity?
items.summary: In his BBC Sport column, ex-Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains why it is so hard for football managers to keep their jobs in the modern game.

items.title: Golfer 'thankful to be alive' after falling down lift shaft in freak accident
items.summary: Italian golfer Andrea Pavan is 'thankful to be alive' after falling three floors down a lift shaft in South Africa, according to a GoFundMe page set up by his family.

items.title: Rhodes stays in hunt, two off lead at Women's World Championship
items.summary: England's Mimi Rhodes remains two shots off leader Auston Kim after the second round of the HSBC Women's World Championship in Singapore.

items.title: Will Aston Villa hold on to their place in the Champions League?
items.summary: Villa’s run of three wins in nine league games has opened the door to Manchester United, Chelsea and Liverpool By WhoScored Two months ago, Aston Villa were just three points off top spot in the Premier League. They were on a run of 12 wins in 14 games that included victories against Manchester City and Arsenal. Their run of eight consecutive wins in the league was their best since they won 10 in a row in 1910. You wouldn’t have blamed Villa fans for daring to dream about lifting their first league title since 1981. But success breeds expectation, and expectation carries its own cruel weight. Arsenal tore Villa apart in a 4-1 win in late December and victories have been hard to come by in the new year – they have only won three of their last nine league games. Liverpool, Manchester United and Chelsea are still below them in the table but the gap is narrowing. With games against United and Chelsea coming in March, the top-three spot Villa have occupied since they beat Wolves in late November suddenly looks in jeopardy. So does their place in the Champions League next season. Continue reading...

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Ferrous and El Bodon clash in Lingfield Sprint Trial
items.summary: We have a busy day's racing on Friday with action from Doncaster, Lingfield, Newbury and Wolverhampton, live on Sky Sports Racing...
